title: ophrys apifera subsp. arachnites
intro: Ophrys apifera subsp. arachnites, also known as the spider orchid, is a subspecies of wild orchid native to the Mediterranean region. This plant is known for its unique appearance, with a yellow and brown coloration resembling a spider's web, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The spider orchid is said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be beneficial for treating wounds and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the spider orchid can be used to make teas, infusions, or salves, but further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
